name: Paragon Embedded Integration Platform REST API
what_it_does: Paragon embedded integration platform REST API for SaaS companies to build and deploy native integrations for their customers — enabling AI agents to manage integration workflows, configure user-facing connectors, trigger automation tasks, and monitor integration health through Paragon's white-label embedded iPaaS platform that SaaS companies deploy within their own products. Enables AI agents to manage integration management for user-facing integration workflow creation and configuration automation, handle workflow execution for integration workflow trigger and execution status automation, access connected account management for end-user integration credential and connection lifecycle automation, retrieve event management for integration trigger event processing and workflow automation, manage task management for individual workflow task status and retry automation, handle connector management for available integration connector configuration automation, access monitoring management for integration health, error rate, and execution analytics automation, retrieve user management for per-user integration configuration and personalization automation, manage environment management for staging and production integration environment automation, and integrate Paragon with SaaS product APIs for embedded native integration delivery automation.
use_cases: Triggering customer integration workflows from SaaS product events for embedded automation agents
Managing end-user connected account credentials for native integration configuration automation agents
Monitoring integration workflow health and execution errors for SaaS platform reliability agents
Building user-facing integration configuration experiences for SaaS product integration automation agents
not_for: Enterprise internal integration without SaaS product context (Paragon is embedded iPaaS for SaaS products; Workato and MuleSoft serve internal enterprise integration)
Consumer-facing automation workflows (Paragon is B2B SaaS native integrations; Zapier and Make serve consumer/prosumer automation)
API management and gateway (Paragon manages integrations, not API traffic; Kong and Apigee serve API gateway needs)
best_when: A B2B SaaS company wanting AI agents to manage embedded native integrations for customers — triggering workflows, managing connections, and monitoring integration health — through Paragon's white-label embedded iPaaS delivered within the SaaS product.
avoid_when: PARAGON IS FOR SAAS BUILDERS NOT END USERS: Paragon is built for SaaS companies to embed integrations in their products; automated end-user assumption creates architecture_mismatch for organizations expecting a Zapier-like user automation tool; automated is the SaaS company building the integration experience for their customers. USER CREDENTIAL MANAGEMENT IS DELEGATED: Paragon manages end-user OAuth tokens for connected integrations; automated direct-credential assumption creates auth_mismatch for implementations trying to manage OAuth tokens directly instead of through Paragon's connected account API; automated must use Paragon's connected account system. WORKFLOW DESIGN IS DONE IN PARAGON DASHBOARD: Integration workflows are designed in Paragon's visual builder, not created via API; automated api-only-design assumption creates no_workflow for attempting to create workflow logic via API without prior visual builder design; automated must design workflows in Paragon dashboard before API trigger. PARAGON USER CONCEPT IS YOUR CUSTOMER: Paragon user ID corresponds to your SaaS product customer; automated generic-user assumption creates isolation_error for workflows not properly scoped to correct Paragon user (your customer); automated must include correct user ID for customer-scoped operations.

auth.notes: Paragon uses API key and JWT for Embedded iPaaS REST API. REST API with JSON. San Francisco, CA HQ. Founded 2020 by Brandon Foo and Ishmael Samuel. Raised $13M+. Products: Paragon Connect (embedded integrations), Paragon Workflows (automation), 100+ pre-built connectors. Embedded iPaaS for SaaS companies. Customers: SaaS companies who embed Paragon in their products for their B2B customers. Competes with Cyclr, Prismatic, and Tray Embedded for embedded iPaaS.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free developer plan: limited workflows and connected accounts
pricing.paid_tiers: Starter, Growth, Enterprise monthly subscription; based on number of integrations, executions, and connected accounts
pricing.notes: San Francisco CA. $13M raised. Developer free plan. SaaS embedded integration pricing. 100+ connectors.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per API key; workflow execution throttled per plan

known_agent_gotchas: PARAGON USER JWT IS REQUIRED FOR USER-SCOPED CALLS: Paragon API calls scoped to a specific end user (your customer) require a signed JWT; automated API-key-only assumption creates user_scope_error for user-specific operations without JWT; automated must sign user-scoped JWTs using your Paragon signing secret

WORKFLOW TRIGGER REQUIRES WORKFLOW ID: Triggering a Paragon workflow requires the workflow's ID (configured in Paragon dashboard); automated dynamic-workflow assumption creates workflow_not_found for trigger calls without pre-configured workflow ID; automated must capture workflow IDs from Paragon dashboard configuration

CONNECTED ACCOUNTS REQUIRE USER AUTHORIZATION: End-user integration connections require user OAuth authorization; automated pre-connected assumption creates integration_not_connected for workflows triggering on users who haven't connected the integration; automated must check connected account status before workflow execution

WORKFLOW EXECUTIONS ARE ASYNCHRONOUS: Paragon workflow executions are asynchronous; automated sync-result assumption creates no_result for execution result retrieval immediately after trigger; automated must use task monitoring or webhook callbacks for execution result tracking

CONNECTOR AVAILABILITY VARIES BY PLAN: Paragon's 100+ connector library has plan-based availability; automated all-connectors assumption creates connector_not_available for integrations requiring connectors not in plan; automated must verify connector availability in plan before building integration
agent_readiness.security_notes: Embedded iPaaS. SOC2. API key + JWT. US. SaaS customer integration credential and workflow data.

security_score_components.security_notes: Embedded iPaaS. SOC2. API key + JWT. US. End-user OAuth credentials and integration workflow data.
